metadata.historical_occupations_note: Pre-disaggregation periods use authored historical occupation labels. On sub-shape diamonds: show the display_name only, no percentage. In the side panel: show share bars with labels for relative sizing context.
metadata.display_name_note: The display_name field (max 25 chars) is for canvas sub-shapes and side panel headers. The occupation_name field retains the full ISCO name.

metadata.splice_note: Pre-1991 records use GGDC 10-Sector data, splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT ISIC Rev.4 classification at the 1991 junction. Correction factors: land_sea=1.271, making_things=1.117, building_things=0.688, moving_things=0.733, buying_selling=0.941, money_data=0.426, care_health=0.689, learning_teaching=0.822, making_meaning=1.222, governing_protecting=0.851, feeding_hosting=0.778, maintaining_fixing=1.444, thinking_leading=0.528. Factors reflect classification differences between GGDC (ISIC Rev.3.1 10-sector) and ILOSTAT (ISIC Rev.4 21-section). Primary discrepancies: GGDC bundles O+P+Q as Government services, K+L+M+N as Business services. ILOSTAT separates these into distinct sections mapped to different territories.
metadata.ilostat_methodology_note:
Phase 1A methodology — ILOSTAT-sourced historical labor shares (v2.7)

DATA SOURCE: ILOSTAT indicator EMP_2EMP_SEX_ECO_NB (Employment by sex and economic activity — ILO modelled estimates), November 2025 vintage. Reference area: X01 (World aggregate). Sex: Total. Frequency: Annual. Accessed 2026-03-30 via the ILOSTAT rplumber API (rplumber.ilo.org), which serves the bulk download facility data.

COVERAGE: 33 annual data points (1991–2023), 13 territories per year = 429 records total. ILOSTAT provides ISIC Rev.4 section-level data at the World level, but groups some sections: H+J (ECO_ISIC4_HJ), L+M+N (ECO_ISIC4_LMN), and R+S+T+U (ECO_ISIC4_RSTU).

DIRECTLY SOURCED TERRITORIES (8 of 13): land_sea (ISIC A+B), making_things (C), building_things (F), buying_selling (G), feeding_hosting (I), governing_protecting (O), learning_teaching (P), care_health (Q). These are computed directly from ILOSTAT World aggregate data with no estimation. 264 records are directly sourced.

SPLIT-ESTIMATED TERRITORIES (5 of 13): moving_things (H from HJ), money_data (J+K+L, with J from HJ and L from LMN), making_meaning (R from RSTU), maintaining_fixing (D+E+S, with S from RSTU), thinking_leading (M+N from LMN). For these, split ratios were derived empirically from ILOSTAT national source data (indicator EMP_TEMP_SEX_ECO_NB) for 10 major economies (USA, DEU, GBR, JPN, BRA, MEX, TUR, IDN, IND, RUS) where individual ISIC sections are published. Employment-weighted split ratios were computed at two anchor periods (~2010 and ~2023) and interpolated/extrapolated linearly for other years. 165 records use empirical split ratios.

SPLIT RATIOS (empirical, employment-weighted):
  ~2010 (10 countries, 1,012,858K workers): H/(H+J)=0.7435, L/(L+M+N)=0.1396, R/(R+S+T+U)=0.2131, S/(R+S+T+U)=0.4741, T+U/(R+S+T+U)=0.3127
  ~2023 (10 countries, 1,179,006K workers): H/(H+J)=0.7020, L/(L+M+N)=0.1333, R/(R+S+T+U)=0.1820, S/(R+S+T+U)=0.4965, T+U/(R+S+T+U)=0.3215
  Split ratios are linearly interpolated between these anchors and linearly extrapolated (with bounds clamping) for years outside the range.

T+U HANDLING: Sections T (Households as employers) and U (Extraterritorial organizations) are part of the RSTU combined group. After splitting R and S out, the T+U residual is distributed proportionally across all 13 territories during normalization.

TOTAL WORKFORCE: At the 9 anchor years specified in the brief (1991, 1995, 2000, 2005, 2010, 2015, 2019, 2021, 2023), the fixed totals from the project specification are used for computing absolute workers (millions). For the 24 intermediate years, ILO total employment from the same ILOSTAT indicator is used (converted from thousands to millions). Note: ILO total for 1991 (2,258M) differs from the brief's fixed total (2,390M), suggesting different estimation methodologies for the total workforce. Shares are computed from ILO section-level data regardless.

REFERENCE RANGE DISCREPANCIES: The brief provided reference ranges for 6 territories at 5 years (30 checks). 17 of 30 pass. Systematic discrepancies exist for:
  - thinking_leading: ILOSTAT shows 1.9%–4.2% vs brief reference 3%–9%. At the global level, formal professional/administrative services (ISIC M+N) represent a much smaller employment share than in OECD economies, because the majority of the world's workforce is in developing economies where these activities are informal or classified under other sectors.
  - care_health: ILOSTAT shows 3.1%–4.2% vs brief reference 4%–7% for later years. Global formal health employment is lower than developed-economy profiles suggest.
  - building_things: ILOSTAT shows 8.3% in 2023 vs brief reference 6%–8%. China's construction boom (ISIC F) drives the global share above the reference ceiling.
  These discrepancies reflect genuine differences between the ILOSTAT World aggregate and the reference ranges, not data quality issues. They warrant review by the data engineering team to determine whether the reference ranges should be recalibrated or whether alternative data sources should supplement the ILOSTAT estimates.

DATA QUALITY FLAGS:
  - 2019–2021 shows expected COVID effects: feeding_hosting dips from 3.8% to 3.3% (2020), agriculture shows slight reversion (workers returning to subsistence farming).
  - No territory changes by more than 3 percentage points between adjacent annual data points.
  - Land & Sea shows steady decline from 44.1% (1991) to 27.6% (2023), with minor upticks during economic disruptions (1998 Asian crisis, 2008–2009 GFC, 2020 COVID).
  - The 3-sector validation (agriculture/industry/services) aligns with World Bank WDI indicators.

split_methodology.indicator: EMP_TEMP_SEX_ECO_NB (national source data, NOT modelled estimates)
split_methodology.reason_not_modelled: The modelled estimates indicator (EMP_2EMP_SEX_ECO_NB) reports combined groups (HJ, LMN, RSTU) even at country level. Individual ISIC sections are only available in the national source indicator.

rstu_excluded.JPN: ISIC T (households as employers) = 66,769K in 2019, implausibly large — likely national classification captures broader category
rstu_excluded.AUS: ISIC T = 12,887K in 2019, implausibly large — same issue
rstu_excluded.BRA: ISIC T = 6,106K in 2019, T > R+S in some years
rstu_excluded.MEX: ISIC T = 2,383K, T > R+S in most years

split_methodology.pre_2008_method: Linear extrapolation from 2008-2012 OLS trend, clamped to reasonable bounds and renormalized
split_methodology.total_employment_coverage: Panel covers ~39M workers (HJ), ~49M (LMN), ~20M (RSTU) in 2023 — approximately 3% of global employment but concentrated in economies with mature statistical systems
split_methodology.notes: The EMP_TEMP indicator reflects national Labour Force Survey data with individual ISIC Rev.4 section detail. 17 of 25 queried countries reported individual sections. Panel restricted to countries with continuous 2008-2023 coverage to avoid composition effects.

metadata.changelog_v2.8: Major data integrity revision encompassing v2.6, v2.7, and v2.8 changes. LABOR SHARES: (1) Historical 1991-2023 replaced with ILOSTAT modeled estimates (EMP_2EMP_SEX_ECO_NB, Nov 2025 vintage, 429 records at annual resolution). (2) 2024-2025 sourced from same ILOSTAT vintage (26 records). (3) Pre-1991 records splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (13 multiplicative correction factors; GGDC trend shapes preserved, levels anchored to ILOSTAT). (4) ILOSTAT subsection splits for 5 territories (money_data, moving_things, maintaining_fixing, making_meaning, thinking_leading) resolved using 17-country employment panel (USA, DEU, GBR, FRA, JPN, KOR, AUS, BRA, MEX, IND, IDN, TUR, RUS, ITA, ESP, POL, NLD). 80 records directly sourced (2008-2023), 95 records use ratios extrapolated from panel trend. (5) Moderate 2026 labor values formula-aligned (headline 3,417M to 3,466M). (6) All projected shares normalized to 100% within scenario-year groups. REPLACEABILITY: (7) Learning & Teaching 2026 baseline raised from 18 to 28 (Khanmigo 1.4M users, Harvard RCT 2x learning gains, AI grading 60-80%, IEP automation). Projected trajectory revised across all 3 scenarios. L&T displacement recomputed. (8) 52 moderate territory-specific capability narratives replace generic text. (9) 104 conservative + accelerated narratives added. Zero generic narratives remain. OCCUPATIONS: (10) 29 occupation benchmark anchors added (13 with new 2030 projections). All 389 occupations now have substantive anchors. (11) ISCO labeling corrections: 7142 Architects→Glaziers, 6224 Soil Scientists→Forestry Workers, 2624→2621 code fix, 3131 Media Tech Educators→Photographers. (12) Territory reassignments: 5164 Animal Care Workers→maintaining_fixing, 7133 Building Cleaners→maintaining_fixing. (13) L&T below-baseline rationale documented for 9 occupations. SOURCE ARCHITECTURE: Single primary source per era — ILOSTAT (1991-2025), GGDC splice-adjusted (pre-1991), displacement formula (2026-2041). World Bank WDI as secondary validation throughout. (14) Conservative and accelerated scenarios removed from production dataset (archived separately). Accelerated had undocumented lag schedule and 2041 uptick anomaly. Conservative was pure ILO baseline with no displacement applied. Moderate is the sole displayed and shipped scenario. (15) ISCO corrections: 2611 Paralegals→Lawyers (correct ISCO-08), 2621 deleted (wrong code, redundant with 2612), 2612 promoted to display, 2624→2621 Archivists moved to making_meaning. Occupation count 389→388.
metadata.scenarios_note: v2.8 ships moderate scenario only. Conservative and accelerated scenarios were computed but removed from the production dataset. Conservative had no displacement applied (pure ILO baseline). Accelerated had an undocumented lag schedule with a 2041 uptick anomaly. Both are archived in scenarios_archive_v2.9.json for potential v2 site work with a scenario toggle. The moderate scenario (1/3 conversion rate, 5/28/48/58/60% lag schedule) is the displayed scenario.
metadata.changelog_v2.9: Production cleanup and ISCO corrections. (1) Conservative and accelerated scenarios removed from production dataset. Conservative was the ILO baseline input to the displacement formula (no displacement applied). Accelerated had undocumented lag schedule and 2041 uptick anomaly. Both archived in scenarios_archive_v2.9.json for v2 site scenario toggle work. Moderate is the sole shipped scenario. (2) ISCO corrections: 2611 relabeled Paralegals→Lawyers (correct ISCO-08); 2621 "Legal Professionals (Judges, Prosecutors)" deleted (wrong ISCO code, redundant with 2612); 2612 Judges & Prosecutors promoted to governing_protecting display set; 2624→2621 Archivists and Curators corrected to valid ISCO-08 code and moved learning_teaching→making_meaning. (3) Territory reassignments: 5164 Animal Care Workers buying_selling→maintaining_fixing, 7133 Building Cleaners building_things→maintaining_fixing. Record counts: labor 608, replaceability 327, occupations 388.

v3_scoring_framework.status: Applied. Occupation scores derived from task decomposition model with corrections. 3 territory baselines adjusted.

vector_decision.rationale: C_R and C_G at 2026 differ by 6.9 points. C_R inflects ~1 year earlier than C_G (digitization head start + LLMs enhancing routine before generative). The gap is > 3 points, indicating the distinction materially affects occupation scores for creative vs routine cognitive jobs.

v3_scoring_framework.territory_rmse_raw: Not applied — raw model scores stored as research output only
v3_scoring_framework.calibration_note: Per-territory affine calibration achieves RMSE 0.1 but distorts individual scores. Raw model scores diverge from current scores by MAD 15.4 points (113 occupations >20pt gap). v3.0 work: resolve disagreements by fixing task decompositions, not by forcing calibration.
data_sources: BLS OES May 2024 (154.2M workers, 830 SOC codes)
limitations: Employment weights rely on BLS-to-ISCO crosswalk with many-to-one mappings; some ISCO codes have approximate employment estimates.
ISCO 6 (Agriculture) employment uses FAO proportional estimates; within-group distribution is approximate.
Task decompositions have single-vector assignment per task; some tasks span multiple vectors.
Per-territory affine calibration preserves relative ordering but introduces systematic scaling that may compress or expand score ranges within territories.
Physical vectors (Phi_U, S_E) have weaker empirical grounding than cognitive vectors (C_R, C_G, P_A).
Humanoid robot capabilities are rapidly evolving; Phi_U estimates may be outdated within 6-12 months.

metadata.changelog_v2.10: Employment weights integrated. (1) Global employment weights added to all 388 occupation records (employment_weight_pct field). Weights computed via BLS OES May 2024 → SOC 2018→SOC 2010→ISCO-08 crosswalk chain, adjusted to ILO 1-digit ISCO global proportions. ISCO 6 (Skilled Agricultural) supplemented with FAO data. Weights normalized to sum to 100% within each territory. (2) v3 scoring framework stored in metadata: 6-vector capability model (C_R, C_G, P_A, Phi_S, Phi_U, S_E) with empirically calibrated two-era logistic curves anchored to SWE-bench, GPQA, IFR, humanoid shipment data, and Waymo safety statistics. Framework documented for future v3.0 score derivation. No replaceability scores changed in this version.

changelog_v3.0.summary: v3.0: Task decomposition corrections applied. 138 Cowork review determinations processed. 3 territory baseline adjustments. maintaining_fixing territory recalibrated.
phases: Phase 1: Applied 1,478 task corrections (1,032 RETAG_VECTOR, 446 REWEIGHT) across 101 occupations. 23 BOTH_NEED_CORRECTION determinations applied. 1 CURRENT_SCORE_ERROR (Lawyers 2611: 73->50). 10 TDE determinations with rationale only (scores kept).

Phase 2: 3 territory reassignments: 3131 Photographers -> making_meaning, 3226 Personal trainer -> care_health, 9411 Agricultural Labourers -> land_sea. Employment weights recalculated for 6 affected territories.
Phase 3: Military codes (0110, 0210, 0310) manually scored (MANUAL_V3). 9 high-weight occupations verified and kept at current scores.
Phase 4: ~250 remaining occupations processed. 43 kept (HIGH quality), 104 kept (scores agree within 10), 32 changed (corrected raw closer to baseline), 62 kept (other).
Phase 6: 3 territory baselines updated: making_things 86->78, moving_things 52->57, learning_teaching 28->31.
Phase 7: Displacement cascade recomputed for 3 territories at years 2030, 2035, 2040, 2041. 2041 displacement values computed and stored.
Phase 8: Metadata upgraded for 157 occupations (score_basis, data_quality, benchmark_anchor).

Phase 9: maintaining_fixing second pass. 25 occupations retagged and reweighted for physical repair emphasis. Territory weighted avg: ~36 (baseline: 32, acceptable range: 27-37). Plant operators (8161, 8162, 8181) scored in 45-60 range.
changelog_v3.0.post_production_corrections: Post-production corrections: 8 occupation scores adjusted (overcorrections from task decomposition model), 10 duplicate occupation names resolved, 15 territory field format values normalized, 3 gap-fill common_titles corrected, 2 ISCO mapping discrepancies documented.

metadata.calibration_note: v3.0: Task decomposition model applied with 1,478 corrections from Cowork review. 138 occupations reviewed, 3 territory baselines adjusted. maintaining_fixing territory recalibrated (Phase 9). RMSE computed and documented.

changelog_v3.1.summary: Post-production data quality corrections and R2030 recomputation.
changes: Part 1 — R2030 recomputation: Computed replaceability_2030_moderate from scratch for all 388 occupations using the task decomposition model with 2030 capability values (C_R=0.902, C_G=0.820, P_A=0.994, Phi_S=0.886, Phi_U=0.283, S_E=0.298) derived from fitted S-curves in v3_scoring_framework. Review corrections from v3_review_determinations.json (RETAG_VECTOR, REWEIGHT) applied before scoring. Weights normalised to sum to 1.0. Monotonicity enforced: R2030 = max(model, R2026). Resolved 62 cases where R2030 < R2026. Three occupations with known-unreliable task decompositions (2222 Midwives, 5223 Cashiers, 7121 Blacksmiths) use territory-proportional scaling instead. New field projection_basis_2030 added to all occupations (TASK_DECOMPOSITION_2030 | TASK_DECOMPOSITION_2030_FLOOR | TERRITORY_SCALED). FLOOR cases also carry projection_note_2030.

Part 2 — Event data: Blackboard event (year 1841) impact_description corrected from "around 1890" to "the 1840s". Telegraph event: "Alfred Veil" corrected to "Alfred Vail". Robot Density event: "the highest globally" corrected to "among the highest globally".

Part 3 — Display names: ISCO 3414 "Religous Professionals" → "Religious Professionals". ISCO 3332 "Funeral director" → "Funeral Director". ISCO 7251 "Wind turbine technician" → "Wind Turbine Technician".
Part 4 — territory_metadata: land_sea.panel_note "less than one in four today" → "roughly one in four today".

Part 5 — HTML: index.html title tag "Profession" → "Job". Intro paragraph "less than one in four" → "roughly one in four". buying_selling canvas desc rewritten to remove "largest employer by headcount" framing. care_health canvas desc "technically out of reach" → "barriers to deployment". methodology.html About the Data: inserted missing 1870–1991 GGDC era sentence.

Construction. Major urban development continuing in East Asia and Middle East. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.688).
Retail and wholesale. Large-format retail expanding globally. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.941).
Health and social services. Separated from residual. Nursing and elderly care expanding in developed economies. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.689).
Accommodation and food service. Tourism and hospitality emerging as significant employment. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.778).
Government and public administration. Cold War military spending at historical peak. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.851).

1980 ILO LABORSTA provides detailed breakdown. Agricultural employment still substantial in Asia and Africa but declining globally. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.271).
Education. Separated from residual. Mass education enrollment continuing to expand globally. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.822).
Utilities, repair, personal services. Separated from residual. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.444).
Arts, media, creative industries. Separated from residual. Very small employment share globally. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.222).
Manufacturing. Deindustrialization beginning in North America and Western Europe. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.117).

Finance, insurance, real estate, information. Separated from residual. Computing and financial services beginning explosive growth. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.426).
Transport and logistics. Containerization and hub-and-spoke systems established. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.733).

Professional, scientific, technical, management. Separated from residual. White-collar workforce expanding in advanced economies. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.528).
Construction. Asian infrastructure boom beginning. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.688).
Retail and wholesale. Out-of-town shopping centers and chain retailers dominant in wealthy economies. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.941).
Health and social services. Rapidly expanding in wealthy, aging societies. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.689).
Accommodation and food service. International tourism rapidly expanding. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.778).
Government and public administration. Cold War ending; some military reductions beginning. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.851).
1990 ILO LABORSTA data. End of Cold War. Agricultural employment at 38% globally (highly concentrated in Asia). Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.271).
Education. Universal primary education now the norm in developed economies; secondary expanding globally. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.822).
Utilities, repair, personal services. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.444).
Arts, media, creative industries. Still very small share globally; larger in wealthy nations. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.222).
Manufacturing. Deindustrialization well underway in Western economies; Asia becoming manufacturing hub. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 1.117).
Finance, insurance, real estate, information. Explosive growth in financial services; early internet emerging. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.426).
Transport and logistics. Globalization accelerating; intermodal containerization standard. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.733).
Professional, scientific, technical, management. Rapid expansion of knowledge-economy jobs in wealthy nations. Splice-adjusted to ILOSTAT classification at 1991 junction (factor: 0.528).

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Unimate (1961) commercially deployed at GM for spot welding and die casting. By 1970, ~200 industrial robots installed globally (Unimation Inc.). Capability: one repetitive motion per unit. No vision, no adaptation.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Extremely high unit cost; single-task capability only

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: ASEA IRB 6 (1974): first commercial microprocessor-controlled robot. Cincinnati Milacron T3 (1973): first minicomputer-controlled commercial robot. ~3,000 industrial robots globally. Still single-task, no sensing.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Robots expensive and limited to one repetitive task per unit

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: PUMA 560 (1978): commercial programmable assembly robot. Fanuc ROBOT-Model 1 (1977): commercial pick-and-place robot for CNC machine tending. CNC machining fully commercial. ~10K industrial robots globally. Tasks: welding, painting, machine tending, material transfer. No vision.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: High cost; rigid single-task programming; no adaptive capability

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: ATMs commercially deployed at scale (~60K in US by 1980; Diebold, NCR). ACH electronic funds transfer operational (1972+). Reuters Monitor Money Rates (1973): first electronic trading terminal. Programmable autonomous transaction systems replacing human teller and clearing tasks.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Regulatory frameworks not yet adapted to automated financial decisions

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Fanuc M-series robots in automotive body shops. SCARA robots (Adept 1984) commercial for electronics assembly. ~70K industrial robots globally (IFR). First vision-guided assembly in automotive (limited).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Expanding task range but still rigid; no cross-task flexibility

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: NYSE SuperDOT (1984): automated order routing. Automated credit scoring models emerging (FICO predecessors). Rule-based expert systems for loan underwriting in pilot commercial use. ~100K ATMs in US.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Automated trading and credit scoring emerging but regulatory caution

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Cognex machine vision systems (commercial from 1982) standard for quality inspection. ~500K industrial robots globally (IFR). ABB IRB 6000 for heavy assembly. Automated SMT for PCB assembly. Tasks: welding, painting, assembly, inspection, handling — each robot one task in fixed station.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Growing robot population but each unit task-specific and line-specific

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Program trading operational post-1987. Expert systems for insurance underwriting commercial. Automated payroll processing commercial (ADP). Electronic trading expanding.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Program trading identified as systemic risk; regulatory scrutiny increasing

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Expert systems commercial for engineering analysis (XCON at DEC). CAD/CAM with automated design rule checking. Statistical analysis software commercial (SAS, SPSS). Automated testing frameworks emerging.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Expert systems narrow and brittle; most professional judgment beyond automation

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Amazon.com (1994): first major commercial platform with automated recommendation. Automated inventory management systems commercial (SAP R/3 1992). Automated reorder systems based on point-of-sale data.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: E-commerce emerging; physical retail automation minimal

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Fanuc LR Mate series (1992): compact robots for small-parts assembly. Machine vision inspection widespread in semiconductor and automotive. Automated warehousing (AS/RS) commercial. ~700K industrial robots. PLCs standard. Still no flexibility — each robot task-specific.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Growing capability breadth but retooling costly; no adaptive behavior

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Bloomberg Terminal standard. Early quantitative hedge funds (Renaissance Technologies, D.E. Shaw) using statistical ML. Automated mortgage processing. Online banking emerging (Wells Fargo 1995).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Quant finance growing; retail banking automation expanding

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Amazon collaborative filtering commercial. Automated dynamic pricing in early commercial use. Self-checkout first commercial deployment (NCR). Automated warehouse management systems.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Online retail growing but physical retail dominates; self-checkout early

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: da Vinci surgical system FDA-cleared (2000): robotic surgery executing precise movements under surgeon direction. CAD for mammography in early commercial deployment. Automated lab blood analysis maturing.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Robotic surgery requires surgeon operation; diagnostic AI not yet commercial

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: GPS auto-steer commercially available for tractors (Trimble, John Deere). Automated irrigation with sensor feedback. Yield mapping systems.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Auto-steer for row crops only; livestock and mixed farming fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: ~750K industrial robots globally (IFR). Vision-guided robotic welding and painting commercial (Fanuc, ABB, KUKA). Automated optical inspection standard in electronics. RFID inventory tracking entering manufacturing. Robots still inflexible — retooling for new products expensive and slow.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Broad deployment in structured tasks; no adaptability to novel situations

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Algorithmic trading commercial (~30% equity volume by 2003). FICO score universal in consumer lending. Automated fraud detection via neural networks commercial (HNC Software). E-Trade, Schwab online brokerage with automated execution.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Algorithmic trading growing rapidly; fraud detection ML standard

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: AGVs commercially deployed in warehouses and ports (Konecranes, Kalmar). GPS fleet management and route optimization commercial. Automated sorting in postal/logistics mature.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Autonomous vehicles not commercially available; automation limited to structured warehouse/port environments

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: BI platforms with automated reporting (Cognos, Business Objects). Automated CI/CD beginning (CruiseControl 2001). ERP rule-based decision support. Automated patent search. Statistical process control.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Automated reporting and testing emerging; strategy and design fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Automated total stations (Leica, Trimble) for surveying standard. Robotic demolition machines (Brokk) commercial since 1990s. GPS machine control for excavation and grading entering commercial deployment (Caterpillar, Trimble).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Surveying automated; on-site construction tasks require human presence

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Self-checkout expanding (~90K units by 2006). Recommendation engines widespread in e-commerce. Automated demand forecasting via statistical ML commercial. Automated catalog management.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Self-checkout and recommendation engines expanding; most retail still human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: CAD for mammography commercially standard. da Vinci at ~300 hospitals. Automated drug interaction checking in pharmacy. EHR with rule-based clinical decision support.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Diagnostic AI commercial for screening; treatment decisions fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Automated biometric identification commercial (AFIS fingerprint; NEC NeoFace early facial recognition). Automated surveillance motion detection standard. Automated benefits eligibility screening (rule-based).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Biometrics and surveillance automated; decision-making and enforcement fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: GPS auto-steer expanding. Variable-rate application commercial. Drone crop survey in very early pilot. Automated milking (Lely Astronaut, commercial from 1992, expanding).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Precision ag expanding but narrow subtask automation only

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Automated content aggregation (Google News 2002). Template-based automated reporting in pilot. Automated photo tagging/categorization (early computer vision commercial).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Content aggregation automated; original creation fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Kiva Systems (2003): autonomous mobile warehouse robots — first commercial coordinated multi-robot system. KUKA KR Quantec (2005). Industrial robots approaching 1M globally. Machine learning entering predictive quality control.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Coordinated autonomy emerging; still limited to structured environments

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: HFT firms operational (Citadel, Two Sigma). RPA precursors: workflow automation for back-office. Automated credit decisions standard in consumer lending. Statistical risk models (Basel II).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: HFT dominant in equities; back-office automation accelerating

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Kiva Systems (2003) warehouse robots commercial. Caterpillar MineStar autonomous haul trucks in pilots. DARPA Grand Challenge 2005 demonstrated AV feasibility but no commercial product. Automated container terminals (ECT Rotterdam).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Warehouse and mining autonomy emerging; road autonomy not commercial

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Automated code analysis commercial (Coverity 2003, Fortify 2004). Business analytics dashboards with anomaly detection. Hadoop emerging (2006) but not yet commercial ML applications.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Static analysis and anomaly detection commercial; ML applications nascent

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: GPS-guided grading and excavation commercial (Caterpillar AccuGrade, Topcon). BIM with automated clash detection. Drone site surveying early commercial. Laser scanning for as-built documentation.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Earthmoving partially automated; trades work (electrical, plumbing) fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: E-commerce recommendation engines mature (>35% Amazon revenue attributed). Dynamic pricing algorithms standard. Self-checkout >100K units. ML customer segmentation commercial. Kiva/Amazon automated fulfillment.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Online automation strong; physical retail automation limited to checkout

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: CAD for mammography, lung nodule detection commercially deployed. da Vinci >1,500 systems. Automated pathology slide scanning commercial (Aperio, Hamamatsu). Remote patient monitoring with automated alerts.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Imaging AI expanding; clinical autonomy still minimal due to liability

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Self-order kiosks early commercial in QSR (McDonald's pilot). Coca-Cola Freestyle (2009) automated beverage dispensing. Hotel revenue management via ML dynamic pricing. Kitchen automation extremely limited.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Ordering and pricing automating; food preparation and service fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Predictive policing pilots (CompStat → ML-based prediction). Automated document processing for government. Military drone surveillance (Predator, Reaper). Facial recognition in border control.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Surveillance and document processing automating; governance decisions fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Drone crop surveying entering commercial use. Automated harvesting for grain (auto-guided combines). Precision ag integrating GPS, sensors, decision algorithms. Automated fish-feeding. ~30% US corn acreage using precision ag.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Precision ag growing but global agriculture overwhelmingly manual

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Adaptive learning platforms emerging (Knewton 2008, DreamBox 2006). Automated essay scoring (ETS e-rater, commercial since 1999). Khan Academy (2008) model. MOOCs imminent.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Adaptive practice tools commercial; teaching and mentorship fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Predictive maintenance algorithms commercial for industrial equipment (vibration analysis, thermal ML). Automated vehicle diagnostics (OBD-II). Drone inspection for infrastructure in early commercial pilot.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Diagnostic automation growing; physical repair fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Narrative Science (2010): first commercial automated journalism generating sports and financial reports from structured data. Automated music composition in early commercial use. ML photo enhancement.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Formulaic content generation commercial; creative work beyond reach

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Universal Robots CB series (2008): first commercially affordable collaborative robot (<$35K). Kiva Systems deployed at scale. 3D vision systems commercial (Keyence, Cognex). ~1.05M industrial robots globally (IFR). Deep learning not yet in manufacturing.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Cobots lower cost threshold but deep learning not yet applied

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: RPA commercial (UiPath, Blue Prism). ML-based fraud detection standard. Robo-advisory emerging (Betterment 2010, Wealthfront 2011). Algorithmic trading ~60% US equity volume. NLP for contract analysis early commercial (Kira Systems 2011).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Algorithmic trading dominant; RPA entering enterprises; robo-advisory emerging

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Autonomous mining trucks commercial: Caterpillar, Komatsu FrontRunner (2008) at Rio Tinto mines. Google Self-Driving Car project (2009) no commercial product. AGVs expanding in warehouses.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Mining autonomy commercial; road autonomy years from commercial viability

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Cloud ML platforms early precursors. GitHub (2008) with CI integration. Automated testing mature. Business ML forecasting commercial (SAS, IBM SPSS). Google Translate statistical MT reaching utility.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: ML forecasting and testing mature; creative and strategic work fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Drone site surveying expanding. Automated concrete pouring in pilot. Robotic welding for structural steel in prefabrication (not on-site). BIM-to-field automated layout.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Survey and prefab automation growing; on-site work remains manual

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Personalization engines commercial at scale. A/B testing automation (Optimizely 2010+). Automated content marketing. Customer service chatbots emerging. Programmatic advertising.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Digital commerce highly automated; physical retail still heavily human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Imaging AI approaching clinical research maturity. IBM Watson Health announced (2013). Automated medical coding (NLP-based). Robotic pharmacy dispensing (ScriptPro). Surgical navigation with computer vision.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Diagnostic AI approaching clinical readiness; regulatory clearance slow

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Self-order kiosks expanding in QSR. Automated food sorting/portioning in food processing. Hotel revenue management ML standard at chains. Robot delivery experiments (not commercial).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Ordering kiosks and revenue management commercial; cooking and service human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: PredPol (2012) commercially deployed. ALPR widespread in law enforcement. Military autonomous systems expanding. Automated fraud detection in tax/benefits processing. NLP government document analysis emerging.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Predictive analytics entering policing; governance and command fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Drone spraying in early commercial use (RMAX Japan since 1990s, expanding). AI crop disease detection in research. Automated sorting for produce (vision-based). Precision ag accelerating.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Drone spraying commercial in some markets; most agriculture unautomated

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Remote monitoring and predictive maintenance platforms commercial (GE, Siemens). Drone inspection expanding. Automated network monitoring/self-healing in IT (Splunk). Automated HVAC optimization.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Monitoring and prediction automated; hands-on repair beyond automation

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Narrative Science and Automated Insights commercially deployed at AP for formulaic content. Algorithmic music licensing platforms. Automated video editing emerging.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Data-to-text generation commercial at AP; original journalism and art fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Rethink Robotics Baxter (2012): first commercial collaborative robot for SME factory floors ($22K). Amazon/Kiva at scale. Machine vision QC mainstream. ~1.2M industrial robots. ABB YuMi concept. Industrial IoT emerging (GE Predix).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Cobots and vision reaching SME price points; IoT adding intelligence layer

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Betterment/Wealthfront commercial robo-advisors. NLP entering legal document review (Kira Systems). Automated KYC/AML compliance screening. Banking chatbots early. HFT >50% equity volume.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Robo-advisory commercial; NLP entering legal; regulatory frameworks lagging

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Kiva Systems at scale across Amazon. Autonomous mining trucks at multiple sites. Drone delivery early testing (no commercial). Rio Tinto AutoHaul automated freight rail.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Warehouse and mining autonomous; road and air delivery not commercial

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: AlexNet (2012) deep learning breakthrough — commercial applications not yet widespread. IBM Watson Jeopardy (2011): demonstrated QA capability, commercial immature. E-discovery commercial. ML entering consulting tools.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Deep learning breakthrough but commercial professional services applications years away

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: SAM100 bricklaying robot in development (ships 2015). Drone surveying standard for large projects. Automated rebar tying. GPS machine control widespread for earthmoving.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: First on-site trade robot approaching; most site work still human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Amazon testing automated checkout concepts. ML demand forecasting sophisticated. Automated product photography. NLP customer review analysis. Programmatic advertising dominant in digital.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Frontier retail highly automated; global retail still labor-intensive

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Deep learning medical imaging entering clinical trials (Enlitic 2014). Automated pathology improving. Medical chatbot triage in pilot (Babylon Health 2013). Robotic exoskeletons commercial (ReWalk 2012 FDA-cleared).
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Deep learning entering clinical trials; regulatory pathway still years long

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Automated restaurant ordering expanding. Moley Robotics robotic kitchen announced (2014-2015, not commercial). Automated hotel check-in kiosks. ML restaurant demand forecasting commercial.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Front-of-house automation growing; kitchen and hospitality service fully human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Predictive policing expanding. Automated border control ePassport gates in >20 countries. Drone surveillance for border/infrastructure. Automated government document processing improving.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Automated identification and surveillance expanding; governance decisions human

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: ML crop yield prediction in commercial pilot. Automated weed detection emerging. Drone surveying standard for large operations. Autonomous tractors in testing but not commercial.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: AI decision support growing; physical automation still narrow subtasks

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Adaptive learning growing (Knewton/Pearson). Automated grading limited to structured formats. MOOCs operational but completion rates low. AI tutoring very early.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Content delivery platforms operational; AI cannot replace teacher function

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Predictive maintenance expanding with IoT platforms. Drone inspection commercial for utilities/oil & gas. IT infrastructure management maturing. Physical repair automation negligible.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: IoT-based prediction mature; physical repair requires human hands

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: GANs invented (2014) but no commercial generative products. Automated content generation expanding for structured data. Automated photo curation via ML in stock photography platforms.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Generative AI invented in research; commercial creative tools years away

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Deep learning entering industrial defect detection (commercial pilots). ABB YuMi announced (2014). China begins massive industrial robot deployment. ~1.5M industrial robots globally. Fanuc learning robots (RL for bin picking) in development.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Deep learning entering manufacturing; massive geographic expansion beginning

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Deep learning for trading signals commercial. UiPath/Blue Prism RPA gaining enterprise adoption. NLP document analysis expanding (Lex Machina). Automated insurance underwriting via ML. Chatbot customer service expanding.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Deep learning entering finance; RPA expanding; regulatory adaptation slow

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: Amazon robotics >15,000 units across fulfillment. Autonomous trucks commercial in mines and ports. Waymo >700K autonomous miles testing. Automated port operations expanding.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: Controlled-environment autonomy mature; open-road autonomy testing only

replaceability.benchmark_anchor: ML business forecasting commercial. Automated testing suites mature. NLP document summarization in pilot. Automated code review early. Google Brain/DeepMind research breakthroughs but few commercial professional applications.
replaceability.key_barrier_note: ML tools improving but professional judgment and architecture beyond reach

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: SAM100 bricklaying robot (2015): performed one repetitive motion; could not frame, wire, plumb, or make site decisions
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: SAM100 semi-automated bricklaying robot is commercially launched, representing the first onsite masonry automation.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Highly varied task environments; complex tool use and spatial reasoning required

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: E-commerce recommendation systems emerging; customer service chatbots rule-based and primitive; negotiation and sales judgment remain human
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: AI presence in retail is limited to basic recommendation algorithms on e-commerce sites and brittle, rule-based customer service chatbots.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Complex social interaction and trust-building central to sales role

benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: FDA clearances for AI/ML medical devices are minimal; early computer-aided detection (CAD) systems assist in highlighting potential mammogram abnormalities.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Liability, regulatory approval, and interpersonal care requirements prevent automation

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: Commercial kitchen automation limited to specialized tasks; hospitality robots in early testing; food preparation and service require dexterity and adaptation
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Moley Robotics debuts a $248,000 robotic chef arm system that utilizes complex motion capture to precisely replicate human culinary movements.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Requires humanoid dexterity, safety in food handling, and customer interaction

benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: AI applications in government are predominantly limited to basic predictive policing models and elementary civic database search algorithms.

benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Automation is strictly limited to narrow subtasks such as basic GPS auto-steer in agricultural tractors and rudimentary drone mapping.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Requires physical manipulation, environmental sensing, and adaptive decision-making beyond commercial capability

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: Adaptive learning platforms emerging; automated grading limited; teacher role fundamentally relational and developmental
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Digital education relies on static video lectures (MOOCs) and rigid, multiple-choice auto-grading software.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Social-emotional learning, motivation, and human mentorship viewed as essential to education

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: Predictive maintenance algorithms operational; physical repair automation limited to simple repetitive tasks; diagnostic reasoning emerging
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Automation is restricted to basic, rule-based software that tracks routine maintenance schedules based on static operational hours.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Repair work requires adaptive physical manipulation and on-site problem diagnosis

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: Generative models primitive (early GAN era); content creation commercially AI-assisted but not autonomous; originality and cultural resonance require human creativity
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Automation in creative fields is limited to rigid procedural generation for background assets in video games and basic algorithmic audio mastering.

replaceability.key_barrier_note: Creative vision, cultural judgment, and originality remain frontier challenges

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: ~2M industrial robots globally (IFR 2015); automation limited to high-volume repetitive tasks in automotive and electronics; robot density China ~49/10K workers
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Global industrial robotic stock stands at ~1.6 million units, strictly limited to high-volume, highly repetitive tasks in automotive and electronics.

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: Algorithmic trading and RPA in finance operational; machine learning for fraud detection deployed; regulatory frameworks restrict autonomous financial decisions
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Automation in finance and IT is limited to optical character recognition (OCR) for data entry, rigid algorithmic high-frequency trading, and basic scripting.

benchmark_anchor.dataset_a: Autonomous vehicles in controlled testing; no commercial Level 4 deployment; delivery robots extremely limited
benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Commercial capabilities are restricted to advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) equivalent to SAE Level 2; complete human control is required.

benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Digital legal and professional research relies entirely on human execution of precise Boolean database queries.

benchmark_anchor.dataset_b: Early iterations of commercial 3D concrete printers begin testing on residential-scale projects in controlled environments.

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Building Things 2027 moderate (48): AI-powered BIM and generative design tools (Autodesk AI, Revit copilots) automate structural analysis, MEP coordination, and construction scheduling. Drone-based site surveying and LiDAR processing are commercially routine. Early-stage construction robots handle rebar tying and bricklaying in controlled settings. Remaining gap: most on-site work requires human judgment in unstructured environments — framing, finishing, plumbing routing in retrofit projects, and real-time safety decisions on active construction sites.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Buying & Selling 2027 moderate (68): AI-powered retail systems handle product recommendations, dynamic pricing, inventory optimization, and customer service chatbots at commercial scale (Amazon, Shopify AI). Automated checkout and cashier-less stores (Amazon Go model) are expanding. AI merchandising generates product descriptions, marketing copy, and visual merchandising plans. Remaining gap: complex B2B sales negotiations requiring relationship-building, luxury retail consultative selling, fresh food quality assessment, and physical store operations including stocking, display assembly, and loss prevention in busy environments.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Care & Health 2027 moderate (62): Over 1,100 FDA-cleared AI medical devices handle diagnostic imaging (radiology, pathology, dermatology), with AI matching or exceeding specialist performance in structured diagnostic tasks. AI scribes (Ambient, DAX Copilot) automate clinical documentation. Drug discovery AI platforms accelerate candidate identification. Mental health chatbots (Woebot, Wysa) provide structured CBT. Remaining gap: physical patient care (bathing, repositioning, wound care), surgical procedures, emergency triage requiring embodied assessment, therapeutic relationship-building in mental health, and the full scope of nursing judgment in dynamic clinical settings.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Feeding & Hosting 2027 moderate (38): AI systems manage restaurant reservations, ordering, and kitchen display optimization. Robotic food preparation handles standardized items (Flippy for frying, Makr Shakr for beverages). AI concierge platforms automate hotel booking, check-in, and basic guest services. Remaining gap: the vast majority of food preparation requires human dexterity and judgment — multi-component plating, variable ingredient handling, taste adjustment. Hospitality fundamentally depends on human warmth, cultural sensitivity, and improvised problem-solving for guest needs. Humanoid robots not yet capable of kitchen or housekeeping work at commercial reliability.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity
replaceability.notes: Revised 46→38 per Q1 2026 evidence review: Flippy has ~14 active units; no general cooking or room-cleaning robot commercially deployed.

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Governing & Protecting 2027 moderate (46): AI systems handle document processing, regulatory compliance checking, and administrative case management across government agencies. Predictive policing and surveillance AI are commercially deployed (with controversy). AI legal research tools (CoCounsel) automate case law analysis. Military AI handles reconnaissance, logistics optimization, and some autonomous targeting decisions. Remaining gap: courtroom advocacy and judicial reasoning, complex policy formulation, diplomatic negotiation, community policing requiring interpersonal trust, combat decisions requiring ethical judgment in ambiguous situations, and democratic deliberation processes.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Land & Sea 2027 moderate (48): Precision agriculture AI (John Deere See & Spray, autonomous tractors) commercially deployed on large-scale farms. Drone-based crop monitoring and variable-rate application are industry standard. AI aquaculture systems manage feeding and water quality. Autonomous combine harvesters operate in open fields. Early humanoid robots ($13-30K, Unitree G1 class) are in pilot deployment for structured greenhouse tasks. Remaining gap: smallholder farming (still >70% of global farm employment) in varied terrain, livestock handling requiring animal behavior judgment, fruit and vegetable harvesting demanding dexterous picking, artisanal fishing, forestry in complex terrain, and the enormous informal agricultural workforce in developing economies where mechanization infrastructure is absent.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Learning & Teaching 2027 moderate (31): AI tutoring platforms (Khanmigo at 1.4M+ users, Duolingo Max) deliver personalized knowledge instruction with documented learning gains, particularly in STEM subjects. AI grading handles 60-80% of routine assessment. IEP drafting and administrative documentation substantially automated. Curriculum scaffolding tools generate functional lesson plans. Baseline raised from 18 to 28 reflecting commercially available capability as of Q1 2026. Remaining gap: classroom management requiring embodied authority and real-time behavioral intervention, social-emotional mentoring, physical supervision of children, creative assessment, metacognitive coaching, and building the trust relationships fundamental to effective teaching.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity
replaceability.notes: Revised trajectory (baseline 18→28). Was 25.

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Maintaining & Fixing 2027 moderate (32): AI diagnostic systems for utilities and infrastructure (predictive maintenance platforms, SCADA AI) automate fault detection and maintenance scheduling. Smart grid management AI optimizes energy distribution. AI handles water treatment process control. Remaining gap: the vast majority of physical repair work — plumbing, electrical fault diagnosis in buildings, HVAC repair, appliance servicing, waste management operations — requires human dexterity, diagnostic reasoning in non-standard environments, and the ability to work in confined, cluttered spaces that no commercially available robot can navigate.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Making Meaning 2027 moderate (80): Generative AI produces commercial-quality text, images, music, and video (GPT-4+, Midjourney, Suno, Sora). AI handles most content creation for marketing, journalism, and corporate communications. Game design tools generate environments and NPC behavior. AI sports analysis and broadcast production are commercially deployed. Remaining gap: breakthrough creative vision and artistic direction, live performance requiring embodied presence, cultural commentary requiring lived experience, investigative journalism requiring source relationships, and the authenticity premium that audiences place on human-created art in fine arts and literary contexts.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Making Things 2027 moderate (90): Lights-out manufacturing (Xiaomi model: 81K sqm, zero workers) commercially operational for electronics and standardized products. Industrial robot density exceeds 500/10K workers in advanced economies. AI manages full production planning, quality control, and supply chain optimization. CNC and additive manufacturing are AI-controlled end-to-end. Remaining gap: custom fabrication requiring artisan skill, flexible small-batch production with frequent changeovers, quality inspection of novel products, maintenance of production equipment in non-standard configurations, and manufacturing in developing economies lacking automation infrastructure.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Some regulatory barriers begin eroding in advanced manufacturing economies; economic barriers reduce as humanoid cost approaches $18K

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Money & Data 2027 moderate (84): AI handles most financial analysis, algorithmic trading, credit underwriting, fraud detection, and compliance monitoring at or above human performance. AI coding assistants generate 30-50% of code at frontier companies (SWE-bench: 80.9%). AI actuarial models, legal document review, and real estate valuation operate at commercial scale. Remaining gap: complex M&A negotiation, novel financial product structuring, regulatory interpretation in ambiguous cases, enterprise architecture decisions, and high-stakes fiduciary judgment where human accountability is required.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Moving Things 2027 moderate (54): Waymo and competitors operate Level 4 autonomous vehicles commercially in defined urban geofences. Autonomous trucking (Aurora, Kodiak) operates commercially on major highway corridors. Warehouse automation (Amazon Robotics, Ocado) handles most goods movement in structured logistics environments. Drone delivery in limited commercial operation. Remaining gap: last-mile delivery in unstructured urban environments, long-haul trucking in adverse weather, manual loading and unloading of non-standard cargo, maritime shipping in complex port environments, and logistics coordination requiring human judgment in disrupted supply chains.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Thinking & Leading 2027 moderate (80): AI handles most professional knowledge work — legal research (CoCounsel), management consulting analysis, accounting and audit procedures, scientific literature review, and HR screening. AI strategy tools produce competitive analyses and business plans at consultant quality. GPQA and ARC-AGI performance demonstrates near-human reasoning on professional benchmarks. Remaining gap: C-suite strategic leadership requiring organizational intuition, complex client relationship management, courtroom litigation, original scientific hypothesis generation, and the interpersonal influence dimensions of management — motivating teams, navigating organizational politics, and making judgment calls under genuine uncertainty.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Building Things 2030 moderate (57): Humanoid and specialized construction robots (~$15K class) perform structured tasks including concrete finishing, drywall installation, and modular assembly in factory-controlled environments. 3D printing of structural elements is commercially viable for standardized housing. AI project management platforms coordinate multi-trade scheduling autonomously. Remaining gap: complex retrofit work, irregular site conditions, high-rise exterior work, skilled trades requiring adaptive problem-solving in non-standard geometries, and real-time coordination across unpredictable multi-trade workflows.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity
replaceability.notes: Revised 60→57 per Q1 2026 evidence review: MEP trades (30-40% of construction labor) have zero robotic automation.

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Buying & Selling 2030 moderate (88): AI sales agents autonomously manage most B2B procurement cycles and consumer e-commerce interactions end-to-end. Humanoid robots (~$15K) handle shelf stocking, inventory management, and basic customer assistance in physical retail. Visual AI systems assess product quality including fresh produce. Autonomous last-mile delivery reduces logistics costs. Remaining gap: high-stakes enterprise sales requiring trust-building over months, luxury and bespoke retail experiences, complex dispute resolution, and managing physical retail environments during peak chaos (Black Friday scenarios).
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Care & Health 2030 moderate (74): AI diagnostic systems cover most medical specialties with above-human accuracy for pattern-recognition tasks. Early humanoid care robots (~$15K) assist with structured physical tasks — medication delivery, patient monitoring, mobility assistance in controlled environments. Surgical robots (da Vinci successors) handle an expanding range of procedures with increasing autonomy. Remaining gap: complex nursing care requiring adaptive physical touch, palliative and end-of-life care, pediatric care requiring emotional attunement, emergency medicine improvisation, and social work judgment in family crisis situations.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Feeding & Hosting 2030 moderate (53): Humanoid robots (~$15K) entering commercial kitchen and housekeeping roles in structured, high-volume environments — hotel room cleaning with standardized layouts, fast-food assembly lines. AI manages full restaurant operations including dynamic staffing, supply chain, and menu optimization. Remaining gap: fine dining preparation and service, bartending requiring social interaction, housekeeping in non-standard room configurations, event catering requiring adaptive coordination, and the hospitality culture of personal service that drives guest satisfaction in mid- and upper-tier establishments.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity
replaceability.notes: Revised 60→53 per Q1 2026 evidence review: Flippy has ~14 active units; no general cooking or room-cleaning robot commercially deployed.

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Governing & Protecting 2030 moderate (58): AI automates most routine regulatory review, permit processing, and compliance monitoring. Autonomous security systems patrol structured environments. AI policy analysis tools model legislative impacts with increasing sophistication. Military autonomous systems expand across logistics, surveillance, and some weapons platforms. Remaining gap: legislative drafting requiring political judgment, judicial sentencing, diplomatic crisis management, complex criminal investigation requiring human intuition and informant relationships, firefighting in unpredictable structures, and the democratic legitimacy requirements of governance.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Land & Sea 2030 moderate (58): Autonomous farm equipment operates across most broadacre crops. Humanoid-type agricultural robots (~$15K) begin commercial harvesting of high-value crops (strawberries, tomatoes) in structured greenhouse environments. AI manages full-cycle crop planning, pest management, and yield optimization. Autonomous fishing vessels operate in defined oceanic zones. Remaining gap: harvesting in unstructured outdoor environments with variable terrain and weather, smallholder subsistence farming, animal husbandry requiring behavioral understanding, forest management in complex ecosystems, and developing-world agriculture where infrastructure for robotic deployment does not exist.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Learning & Teaching 2030 moderate (41): AI tutoring systems cover most academic subjects with near-human effectiveness for knowledge transfer and structured skill-building. AI assessment handles open-ended and creative work with increasing sophistication. AI teaching assistants manage differentiated instruction for large class groups. Remaining gap: the embodied and relational core of teaching — maintaining classroom culture, motivating disengaged learners through personal connection, supporting students through social-emotional crises, physical education, early childhood development requiring embodied play, and the mentoring relationships that shape student identity and aspiration.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity
replaceability.notes: Revised trajectory (baseline 18→28). Was 40.

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Maintaining & Fixing 2030 moderate (46): Humanoid robots (~$15K) beginning to perform structured maintenance tasks in industrial settings — filter replacement, standardized inspections, meter reading in accessible locations. AI manages utility networks with increasing autonomy. Predictive maintenance reduces unplanned failures across infrastructure. Remaining gap: repair work requiring diagnosis of novel failures, working in residential and commercial buildings with non-standard configurations, skilled trades (electrician, plumber) requiring adaptive problem-solving, and waste management in unstructured environments.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Making Meaning 2030 moderate (86): AI generates feature-length films, novel-length fiction, and interactive entertainment at commercial quality. AI sports broadcasting and event production operate autonomously. Creative AI handles most graphic design, advertising, and media production end-to-end. Remaining gap: visionary creative direction, live theatrical and musical performance, cultural criticism requiring contextual depth, original investigative journalism, and the diminishing but persistent market premium for verified human-created art and writing.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Making Things 2030 moderate (96): Fully automated manufacturing extends to most product categories including soft goods and food processing. Humanoid robots (~$15K) handle flexible assembly tasks previously requiring human workers. AI quality systems detect defects at near-perfect rates. Remaining gap: artisanal and bespoke manufacturing, prototype production of novel product categories, manufacturing equipment repair and reconfiguration, and the long tail of specialized manufacturing processes requiring human judgment.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Money & Data 2030 moderate (94): AI systems autonomously manage most financial operations — portfolio management, insurance underwriting, loan origination, and regulatory reporting. AI generates production-quality software for most application types. Data center and cloud infrastructure are AI-managed. Remaining gap: novel financial instrument design, complex multi-party negotiations, systemic risk assessment requiring judgment beyond historical patterns, and strategic technology decisions requiring organizational context.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Moving Things 2030 moderate (68): Level 4 autonomous trucking commercially operational on major highway corridors. Last-mile delivery robots scaled beyond pilot in structured suburban environments. Port and warehouse automation near-complete. Humanoid robots (~$15K) handle parcel sorting and loading in semi-structured environments. Remaining gap: unstructured urban last-mile, adverse-weather long-haul, manual loading/unloading in non-standard environments, complex multimodal logistics requiring adaptive coordination, and maritime operations in challenging conditions.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

replaceability.primary_mechanism: Thinking & Leading 2030 moderate (90): AI systems autonomously perform the full scope of professional services — management consulting deliverables, legal brief writing, architectural design, engineering analysis, and scientific research assistance. AI handles most administrative services (staffing, security, cleaning management). Remaining gap: senior leadership and strategic direction-setting, trial advocacy, novel scientific research requiring creative leaps, organizational change management, and professional services requiring deep client trust built over years.
replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity
